heading: Art. 25a. Number of court-martial members in capital cases
label: 825a Art. 25a. Number of court-martial members in capital cases
notes: Amendments2016— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In a case in which the accused may be sentenced to a penalty of death, the number of members shall be not less than 12, unless 12 members are not reasonably available because of physical conditions or military exigencies, in which case the convening authority shall specify a lesser number of members not less than five, and the court may be assembled and the trial held with not less than the number of members so specified. In such a case, the convening authority shall make a detailed written statement, to be appended to the record, stating why a greater number of members were not reasonably available.”Pub. L. 114–328

Effective Date of 2016 AmendmentAmendment by effective on , as designated by the President, with implementing regulations and provisions relating to applicability to various situations, see and Ex. children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: In a case in which the accused may be sentenced to death, the number of members shall be 12.
if a case is referred for trial as a capital case and, before the members are impaneled, the accused may no longer be sentenced to death, the number of members shall be eight; and
if a case is referred for trial as a capital case and, after the members are impaneled, the accused may no longer be sentenced to death, the number of members shall remain 12.
